#REDIRECT[[多宝塔]]

'''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、円筒や八角などの塔身に方形などの屋根を被せ、さらに相輪を載せた[[仏塔]]。
== その他の用法 == *[[多宝塔]]などの仏塔を美称して用いられることもある。 *金銀玉などで飾り立てた装飾仏塔のこと。
== 各地の宝塔 ==
== 関連項目 ==

== その他の用法 == *[[多宝塔]]などの仏塔を美称して用いられることもある。 *金銀玉などで飾り立てた装飾仏塔のこと。
== 各地の宝塔 ==
== 関連項目 ==

{{Buddhism}} '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、円筒や八角などの塔身に方形などの屋根を被せ、さらに相輪を載せた[[仏塔]]。[[法華宗]]では、「[[妙法蓮華経]]」見宝塔品第十一に基づき[[釈迦如来]]・[[多宝如来]]を脇士として[[本尊]]とする。

== その他の用法 == *[[多宝塔]]などの仏塔を美称して用いられることもある。 *金銀玉などで飾り立てた装飾仏塔のこと。
== 各地の宝塔 ==
== 関連項目 == *[[一塔両尊]]

[[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（東京都文化財）]]
'''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、円筒や八角などの塔身に方形などの屋根を被せ、さらに相輪を載せた[[仏塔]]。木、石、金属、などの素材を使って造形化されたもの。[[法華宗]]では、「[[妙法蓮華経]]」見宝塔品第十一に基づき[[釈迦如来]]・[[多宝如来]]を[[脇侍|脇士]]として[[本尊]]とする。
== その他の用法 == *[[多宝塔]]などの仏塔を美称して用いられることもある。 *金銀玉などで飾り立てた装飾仏塔のこと。
== 各地の宝塔 == *本門寺（東京都、都有形文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。 ===屋内小塔=== *慈光寺（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *性海寺（愛知県、重要文化財） *長蔵寺（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、都有形文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
== 関連項目 == *[[一塔両尊]]

'''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、円筒や八角などの塔身に方形などの屋根を被せ、さらに相輪を載せた[[仏塔]]。木、石、金属、などの素材を使って造形化されたもの。[[法華宗]]では、「[[妙法蓮華経]]」見宝塔品第十一に基づき[[釈迦如来]]・[[多宝如来]]を[[脇侍|脇士]]として[[本尊]]とする。
== その他の用法 == *[[多宝塔]]などの仏塔を美称して用いられることもある。 *金銀玉などで飾り立てた装飾仏塔のこと。
== 各地の宝塔 == *本門寺（東京都、都有形文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。 ===屋内小塔=== *慈光寺（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *性海寺（愛知県、重要文化財） *長蔵寺（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、都有形文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
== 関連項目 == *[[一塔両尊]] *[[国東塔]]
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほうとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]] [[Category:法華系仏教]] [[Category:法華経]]

[[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（東京都文化財）]]
'''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、円筒や八角などの塔身に方形などの屋根を被せ、さらに相輪を載せた[[仏塔]]。木、石、金属、などの素材を使って造形化されたもの。
[[法華宗]]では、「[[妙法蓮華経]]」見宝塔品第十一に基づき[[釈迦如来]]、[[多宝如来]]の二尊を[[本尊]]として上行菩薩、無辺行菩薩、浄行菩薩、安立行菩薩の[[四菩薩]]を[[脇侍|脇士]]（四士）とする。（これを二尊四士または、二尊四菩薩と言う。）
== その他の用法 == *[[多宝塔]]などの仏塔を美称して用いられることもある。 *金銀玉などで飾り立てた装飾仏塔のこと。
== 各地の宝塔 == *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *慈光寺（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *性海寺（愛知県、重要文化財） *長蔵寺（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、都有形文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
== 関連項目 == *[[一塔両尊]] *[[国東塔]]
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほうとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]] [[Category:法華系仏教]] [[Category:法華経]]

[[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（東京都文化財）]]
'''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、円筒や八角などの塔身に方形などの屋根を被せ、さらに相輪を載せた[[仏塔]]。木、石、金属、などの素材を使って造形化されたもの。
[[法華宗]]では、「[[妙法蓮華経]]」見宝塔品第十一に基づき[[釈迦如来]]、[[多宝如来]]の二尊を[[本尊]]として上行菩薩、無辺行菩薩、浄行菩薩、安立行菩薩の[[四菩薩]]を[[脇侍|脇士]]（四士）とする。（これを二尊四士または、二尊四菩薩と言う。）
== その他の用法 == *[[多宝塔]]などの仏塔を美称して用いられることもある。 *金銀玉などで飾り立てた装飾仏塔のこと。
== 各地の宝塔 == *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *慈光寺（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *性海寺（愛知県、重要文化財） *長蔵寺（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、都有形文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造小塔=== *関寺跡牛塔（滋賀県、重要文化財）石造宝塔としては日本最古 *石山寺目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *六波羅蜜寺阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代
== 関連項目 == *[[一塔両尊]] *[[国東塔]]

revision.comment: 出典に基づき定義部分の整理と記述対象の明確化。本項の記述対象は法華経見宝塔品とは直接関係ない。
revision.text:
[[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ----
'''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の塔身に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。日本の現存作例としては、[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（平安時代後期）が最古である<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。
== 各地の宝塔 == *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *慈光寺（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *性海寺（愛知県、重要文化財） *長蔵寺（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造小塔=== *関寺跡牛塔（滋賀県、重要文化財）石造宝塔としては日本最古 *石山寺目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *六波羅蜜寺阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[国東塔]]
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほうとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

[[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ----
'''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の塔身に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。日本の現存作例としては、[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（平安時代後期）が最古である<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。
== 各地の宝塔 == [[ファイル:Houkakuji-sekizouhoutouReplica-20100605.jpg|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]]〉）]] *[[本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *[[慈光寺]]（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *[[性海寺]]（愛知県、重要文化財） *[[長蔵寺]]（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造小塔=== *[[関寺]]跡牛塔（滋賀県、重要文化財）石造宝塔としては日本最古 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[国東塔]]
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほうとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

[[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ---- == 宝塔の形態と異議 == '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の塔身に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。屋根の上には通常の層塔と同じく相輪を載せる。木造、金属製、石造のものがあるが、木造建築としての例は極めて少ない。日本の現存作例としては、[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（平安時代後期）が最古である<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。なお、円筒部に四角い庇を設けるものが見られる。この庇は裳階であり、教義上宝塔になんら変更を加えるものではないが、便宜上これを「[[多宝塔]]と称して区別している。
== 各地の宝塔 == [[ファイル:Houkakuji-sekizouhoutouReplica-20100605.jpg|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]]〉）]] *[[本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *[[慈光寺]]（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *[[性海寺]]（愛知県、重要文化財） *[[長蔵寺]]（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造小塔=== 石造美術の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤崎塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安]]元年銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡牛塔（滋賀県、重要文化財） 平安末期 高さ3.3ｍ、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[国東塔]]
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほうとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

== 宝塔の例 == ===木造宝塔=== [[ファイル:Houkakuji-sekizouhoutouReplica-20100605.jpg|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]]〉）]] *[[本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *[[慈光寺]]（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *[[性海寺]]（愛知県、重要文化財） *[[長蔵寺]]（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造宝塔=== [[石造美術]]の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色を示す例として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤崎塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安]]元年銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡「牛塔」（滋賀県・重要文化財） 平安末期 高さ3.3ｍ、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。前記鞍馬寺宝塔とともに最古に位置する。 *満願寺跡宝塔（滋賀県高島市）鎌倉前期 高さ4ｍ。相輪は後補。 *[[浄土寺]]宝塔（広島県尾道市・重要文化財） [[弘安]]元年銘 高さ2.8ｍ。檀上積みに立つ。 *[[西明寺]]宝塔（滋賀県甲良町・重要文化財） [[嘉元]]2年銘 *[[石山寺]]石造多宝塔（滋賀県大津市） 鎌倉中期 現状高さは2ｍほどだが元は3ｍほどであったと考えられる。 *[[安養寺]][[慈円]]僧正宝塔（京都市・重要文化財） 鎌倉中期 高さ約3ｍ、塔身に多宝・釈迦二仏を刻む。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代

revision.comment: 読者に誤解を与えかねない説明を修正。多宝塔の初層は裳階ではない。
revision.text:
[[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ---- == 宝塔の形態と意義 == '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の軸部（塔身）に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。円筒の上部を丸く面取りしいわゆる亀腹状にする。屋根の上には通常の層塔と同じく相輪を載せる。木造、金属製、石造のものがあるが、木造建築としての例は極めて少ない。日本の現存作例としては、[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（保安元年頃・平安時代後期）が最古である<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。なお、平面方形の初重の上に平面円形の上重を載せた二重塔婆を「[[多宝塔]]」と称し、形式上「宝塔」と区別しているが、この区別は便宜的なものである。
== 宝塔の例 == ===木造宝塔=== [[ファイル:Houkakuji-sekizouhoutouReplica-20100605.jpg|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]]〉）]] *[[本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。

===石造宝塔=== [[石造美術]]の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色を示す例として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤崎塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安]]元年銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡「牛塔」（滋賀県・重要文化財） 平安末期 高さ3.3m、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。前記鞍馬寺宝塔とともに最古に位置する。 *満願寺跡宝塔（滋賀県高島市）鎌倉前期 高さ4m。相輪は後補。 *[[浄土寺]]宝塔（広島県尾道市・重要文化財） [[弘安]]元年銘 高さ2.8m。檀上積みに立つ。 *[[西明寺]]宝塔（滋賀県甲良町・重要文化財） [[嘉元]]2年銘 *[[石山寺]]石造多宝塔（滋賀県大津市） 鎌倉中期 現状高さは2mほどだが元は3mほどであったと考えられる。 *[[安養寺]][[慈円]]僧正宝塔（京都市・重要文化財） 鎌倉中期 高さ約3m、塔身に多宝・釈迦二仏を刻む。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代

[[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ---- == 宝塔の形態と意義 == '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の軸部（塔身）に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。円筒の上部を丸く面取りしいわゆる亀腹状にする。屋根の上には通常の層塔と同じく相輪を載せる。木造、金属製、石造のものがあるが、木造建築としての例は極めて少ない。日本の現存作例としては、[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（保安元年頃・平安時代後期）が最古である<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。なお、平面方形の初重の上に平面円形の上重を載せた二重塔婆を「[[多宝塔]]」と称し、形式上「宝塔」と区別しているが、この区別は便宜的なものである。
== 宝塔の例 == ===木造宝塔=== [[ファイル:Houkakuji-sekizouhoutouReplica-20100605.jpg|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]]〉）]] *[[本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *[[慈光寺]]（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *[[性海寺]]（愛知県、重要文化財） *[[長蔵寺]]（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造宝塔=== [[石造美術]]の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色を示す例として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤崎塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安 (元号)|保安]]元年（[[1120年]]）銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡「牛塔」（滋賀県・重要文化財） [[平安時代]]末期 高さ3.3m、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。前記鞍馬寺宝塔とともに最古に位置する。 *満願寺跡宝塔（滋賀県高島市）[[鎌倉時代]]前期 高さ4m。相輪は後補。 *[[浄土寺]]宝塔（広島県尾道市・重要文化財） [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）銘 高さ2.8m。檀上積みに立つ。 *[[西明寺]]宝塔（滋賀県甲良町・重要文化財） [[嘉元]]2年（[[1304年]]）銘 *[[石山寺]]石造多宝塔（滋賀県大津市） 鎌倉時代中期 現状高さは2mほどだが元は3mほどであったと考えられる。 *[[安養寺]][[慈円]]僧正宝塔（京都市・重要文化財） 鎌倉時代中期 高さ約3m、塔身に多宝・釈迦二仏を刻む。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代
== 参考文献 == * [[石田茂作]]「塔-塔婆・スツーバ」日本の美術77 1972 至文堂 * [[中西亨]]「日本の塔総観」1970 文華堂 * [[川勝政太郎]]「石造美術入門」1967 社会思想社
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[国東塔]] *[[多宝塔]]
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほうとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

revision.comment: bot: [[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼]]「削除告知」
revision.text:
{{RFD notice|'''対象リダイレクト:'''[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD宝塔院 (院号)|宝塔院 (院号)（受付依頼）]]|※編集上のご注意！このテンプレートはBotによって自動貼付、および審議終了後およそ1週間で自動除去されます。審議途中でリダイレクト先を変更した場合や記事に置き換えた場合、またリダイレクトが削除された後でもテンプレートを取り除かないでください。前後に使用されているnoinclude文も同様です。手動で取り除いた場合、再度自動貼付されることがあります。}} {{otheruses|建築|中国延安市の区|宝塔区}} [[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ---- == 宝塔の形態と意義 == '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の軸部（塔身）に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。円筒の上部を丸く面取りしいわゆる亀腹状にする。屋根の上には通常の層塔と同じく相輪を載せる。木造、金属製、石造のものがあるが、木造建築としての例は極めて少ない。日本の現存作例としては、[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（保安元年頃・平安時代後期）が最古である<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。なお、平面方形の初重の上に平面円形の上重を載せた二重塔婆を「[[多宝塔]]」と称し、形式上「宝塔」と区別しているが、この区別は便宜的なものである。
== 宝塔の例 == ===木造宝塔=== [[ファイル:Houkakuji-sekizouhoutouReplica-20100605.jpg|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]]〉）]] *[[本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *[[慈光寺]]（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *[[性海寺]]（愛知県、重要文化財） *[[長蔵寺]]（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造宝塔=== [[石造美術]]の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色を示す例として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤崎塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安 (元号)|保安]]元年（[[1120年]]）銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡「牛塔」（滋賀県・重要文化財） [[平安時代]]末期 高さ3.3m、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。前記鞍馬寺宝塔とともに最古に位置する。 *満願寺跡宝塔（滋賀県高島市）[[鎌倉時代]]前期 高さ4m。相輪は後補。 *[[浄土寺]]宝塔（広島県尾道市・重要文化財） [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）銘 高さ2.8m。檀上積みに立つ。 *[[西明寺 (滋賀県甲良町)|西明寺]]宝塔（滋賀県甲良町・重要文化財） [[嘉元]]2年（[[1304年]]）銘 *[[石山寺]]石造多宝塔（滋賀県大津市） 鎌倉時代中期 現状高さは2mほどだが元は3mほどであったと考えられる。 *[[安養寺]][[慈円]]僧正宝塔（京都市・重要文化財） 鎌倉時代中期 高さ約3m、塔身に多宝・釈迦二仏を刻む。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代
== 参考文献 == * [[石田茂作]]「塔-塔婆・スツーバ」日本の美術77 1972 至文堂 * [[中西亨]]「日本の塔総観」1970 文華堂 * [[川勝政太郎]]「石造美術入門」1967 社会思想社
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[多宝如来]] *[[七宝]] *[[国東塔]] *[[多宝塔]]
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほうとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

revision.comment: 鳳閣寺宝塔の画像差替え（従前の画像はレプリカ）
revision.text:

{{otheruses|建築|中国延安市の区|宝塔区}} [[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ---- == 宝塔の形態と意義 == '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の軸部（塔身）に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。円筒の上部を丸く面取りしいわゆる亀腹状にする。屋根の上には通常の層塔と同じく相輪を載せる。木造、金属製、石造のものがあるが、木造建築としての例は極めて少ない。日本の現存作例としては、[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（保安元年頃・平安時代後期）が最古である<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。なお、平面方形の初重の上に平面円形の上重を載せた二重塔婆を「[[多宝塔]]」と称し、形式上「宝塔」と区別しているが、この区別は便宜的なものである。
== 宝塔の例 == ===木造宝塔=== [[ファイル:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[黒滝村]]〉）]] *[[本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *[[慈光寺]]（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *[[性海寺]]（愛知県、重要文化財） *[[長蔵寺 (美濃市)|長蔵寺]]（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造宝塔=== [[石造美術]]の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色を示す例として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤崎塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安 (元号)|保安]]元年（[[1120年]]）銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡「牛塔」（滋賀県・重要文化財） [[平安時代]]末期 高さ3.3m、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。前記鞍馬寺宝塔とともに最古に位置する。 *満願寺跡宝塔（滋賀県高島市）[[鎌倉時代]]前期 高さ4m。相輪は後補。 *[[浄土寺]]宝塔（広島県尾道市・重要文化財） [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）銘 高さ2.8m。檀上積みに立つ。 *[[西明寺 (滋賀県甲良町)|西明寺]]宝塔（滋賀県甲良町・重要文化財） [[嘉元]]2年（[[1304年]]）銘 *[[石山寺]]石造宝塔（滋賀県大津市） 鎌倉時代中期 現状高さは2mほどだが元は3mほどであったと考えられる。 *[[安養寺]][[慈円]]僧正宝塔（京都市・重要文化財） 鎌倉時代中期 高さ約3m、塔身に多宝・釈迦二仏を刻む。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代
== 参考文献 == * [[石田茂作]]「塔-塔婆・スツーバ」日本の美術77 1972 至文堂 * [[中西亨]]「日本の塔総観」1970 文華堂 * [[川勝政太郎]]「石造美術入門」1967 社会思想社
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[多宝如来]] *[[七宝]] *[[国東塔]] *[[多宝塔]]
{{日本の建築}} {{Buddhism-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ほうとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/宮瀬陽平|宮瀬陽平]] ([[User talk:宮瀬陽平|会話]]) による ID:90922698 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]] ゆとり教育ど真ん中の少子化世代なのに偏差値47.5のド田舎の大学にしか受からず、しかも浪人か留年か、どこでダブったのか知らんが22で卒業できないとかどこまで努力を怠ってきたんだ？（これは「もしそんな人がいたら」という仮定の下書かれています。したがって、実際にそのような人物が実在するか、特定の個人が該当するかどうかとは無関係です。いかなる人物の個人情報も含んでおらず、以下の投稿は何びとのプライバシーも侵害していません。）
revision.text:

{{otheruses|建築|中国延安市の区|宝塔区}} [[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ---- == 宝塔の形態と意義 == '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の軸部（塔身）に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。円筒の上部を丸く面取りしいわゆる亀腹状にする。屋根の上には通常の層塔と同じく相輪を載せる。木造、金属製、石造のものがあるが、木造建築としての例は極めて少ない。日本の現存作例としては、[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（保安元年頃・平安時代後期）が最古である<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。なお、平面方形の初重の上に平面円形の上重を載せた二重塔婆を「[[多宝塔]]」と称し、形式上「宝塔」と区別しているが、この区別は便宜的なものである。
== 宝塔の例 == ===木造宝塔=== [[ファイル:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[黒滝村]]〉）]] *[[池上本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。

===石造宝塔=== [[石造美術]]の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色を示す例として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤崎塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安 (元号)|保安]]元年（[[1120年]]）銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡「牛塔」（滋賀県・重要文化財） [[平安時代]]末期 高さ3.3m、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。前記鞍馬寺宝塔とともに最古に位置する。 *満願寺跡宝塔（滋賀県高島市）[[鎌倉時代]]前期 高さ4m。相輪は後補。 *[[浄土寺 (尾道市)|浄土寺]]宝塔（広島県尾道市・重要文化財） [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）銘 高さ2.8m。檀上積みに立つ。 *[[西明寺 (滋賀県甲良町)|西明寺]]宝塔（滋賀県甲良町・重要文化財） [[嘉元]]2年（[[1304年]]）銘 *[[石山寺]]石造宝塔（滋賀県大津市） 鎌倉時代中期 現状高さは2mほどだが元は3mほどであったと考えられる。 *[[安養寺]][[慈円]]僧正宝塔（京都市・重要文化財） 鎌倉時代中期 高さ約3m、塔身に多宝・釈迦二仏を刻む。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代

== 宝塔の例 == ===木造宝塔=== [[ファイル:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[黒滝村]]〉）]] *[[池上本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *[[慈光寺 (埼玉県ときがわ町)|慈光寺]]（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *[[性海寺 (稲沢市)|性海寺]]（愛知県、重要文化財） *[[長蔵寺 (美濃市)|長蔵寺]]（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造宝塔=== [[石造美術]]の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色を示す例として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤碕塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安 (元号)|保安]]元年（[[1120年]]）銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡「牛塔」（滋賀県・重要文化財） [[平安時代]]末期 高さ3.3m、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。前記鞍馬寺宝塔とともに最古に位置する。 *満願寺跡宝塔（滋賀県高島市）[[鎌倉時代]]前期 高さ4m。相輪は後補。 *[[浄土寺 (尾道市)|浄土寺]]宝塔（広島県尾道市・重要文化財） [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）銘 高さ2.8m。檀上積みに立つ。 *[[西明寺 (滋賀県甲良町)|西明寺]]宝塔（滋賀県甲良町・重要文化財） [[嘉元]]2年（[[1304年]]）銘 *[[石山寺]]石造宝塔（滋賀県大津市） 鎌倉時代中期 現状高さは2mほどだが元は3mほどであったと考えられる。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[安養寺 (京都市東山区)|安養寺]][[慈円]]僧正宝塔（京都市・重要文化財） 鎌倉時代中期 高さ約3m、塔身に多宝・釈迦二仏を刻む。 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代 *[[長楽寺 (太田市)|長楽寺]]宝塔（群馬県太田市・重要文化財） [[建治]]2年（[[1276年]]）
== 参考文献 == * [[石田茂作]]「塔-塔婆・スツーバ」日本の美術77 1972 至文堂 * [[中西亨]]「日本の塔総観」1970 文華堂 * [[川勝政太郎]]「石造美術入門」1967 社会思想社
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[多宝如来]] *[[七宝]] *[[国東塔]] *[[赤城塔]] *[[多宝塔]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ---..."
revision.text:
{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ----
== Form and Significance of the Treasure Tower == A '''Treasure Tower''' (Hōtō) is a type of Buddhist stupa architecture. While the term is sometimes used to refer to Buddhist stupas in general as a form of reverence, in Japanese architectural terminology, it refers specifically to a single-story stupa with a cylindrical central body (tōshin) and a square roof. <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten" (Dictionary of Japanese Stone Sculpture), Tokyo-do, 1978, p.347; Hisao Mae, "Kokenchiku no Kiso Chishiki" (Basic Knowledge of Ancient Architecture), Kōmura Shikōsha, 1986, p.61</ref>. The upper part of the cylindrical body is rounded in a so-called kamebara (tortoise belly) shape. A finial (sorin), similar to that on pagodas, is placed on top of the roof. Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * [[Prabhūtaratna]] * [[Seven Treasures]] * [[Kunisaki Tower]] * [[Akasaki Tower]] * [[Multi-Treasure Tower]]
{{Japanese Architecture}} {{Buddhism-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Hōtō}} [[Category:Temple architecture]] [[Category:Religious stupas]]

The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

== Related Topics == * [[Prabhūtaratna]] * [[Seven Treasures]] * [[Kunisaki Tower]] * [[Akasaki Tower]] * [[Multi-Treasure Tower]]

Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.

== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).

Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).

{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ----
== Form and Significance of the Treasure Tower == A '''Treasure Tower''' (Hōtō) is a type of Buddhist stupa architecture. While the term is sometimes used to refer to Buddhist stupas in general as a form of reverence, in Japanese architectural terminology, it refers specifically to a single-story stupa with a cylindrical central body (tōshin) and a square roof. <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten" (Dictionary of Japanese Stone Sculpture), Tokyo-do, 1978, p.347; Hisao Mae, "Kokenchiku no Kiso Chishiki" (Basic Knowledge of Ancient Architecture), Kōmura Shikōsha, 1986, p.61</ref>. The upper part of the cylindrical body is rounded in a so-called kamebara (tortoise belly) shape. A finial (sorin), similar to that on pagodas, is placed on top of the roof. Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).

The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ----
== Form and Significance of the Treasure Tower == A '''Treasure Tower''' (Hōtō) is a type of Buddhist stupa architecture. While the term is sometimes used to refer to Buddhist stupas in general as a form of reverence, in Japanese architectural terminology, it refers specifically to a single-story stupa with a cylindrical central body (tōshin) and a square roof. <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten" (Dictionary of Japanese Stone Sculpture), Tokyo-do, 1978, p.347; Hisao Mae, "Kokenchiku no Kiso Chishiki" (Basic Knowledge of Ancient Architecture), Kōmura Shikōsha, 1986, p.61</ref>. The upper part of the cylindrical body is rounded in a so-called kamebara (tortoise belly) shape. A finial (sorin), similar to that on pagodas, is placed on top of the roof. Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).

The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).

{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ----
== Form and Significance of the Treasure Tower == A '''Treasure Tower''' (Hōtō) is a type of Buddhist stupa architecture. While the term is sometimes used to refer to Buddhist stupas in general as a form of reverence, in Japanese architectural terminology, it refers specifically to a single-story stupa with a cylindrical central body (tōshin) and a square roof. <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten" (Dictionary of Japanese Stone Sculpture), Tokyo-do, 1978, p.347; Hisao Mae, "Kokenchiku no Kiso Chishiki" (Basic Knowledge of Ancient Architecture), Kōmura Shikōsha, 1986, p.61</ref>. The upper part of the cylindrical body is rounded in a so-called kamebara (tortoise belly) shape. A finial (sorin), similar to that on pagodas, is placed on top of the roof. Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).

{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ----
== Form and Significance of the Treasure Tower == A '''Treasure Tower''' (Hōtō) is a type of Buddhist stupa architecture. While the term is sometimes used to refer to Buddhist stupas in general as a form of reverence, in Japanese architectural terminology, it refers specifically to a single-story stupa with a cylindrical central body (tōshin) and a square roof. <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten" (Dictionary of Japanese Stone Sculpture), Tokyo-do, 1978, p.347; Hisao Mae, "Kokenchiku no Kiso Chishiki" (Basic Knowledge of Ancient Architecture), Kōmura Shikōsha, 1986, p.61</ref>. The upper part of the cylindrical body is rounded in a so-called kamebara (tortoise belly) shape. A finial (sorin), similar to that on pagodas, is placed on top of the roof. Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ----
== Form and Significance of the Treasure Tower == A '''Treasure Tower''' (Hōtō) is a type of Buddhist stupa architecture. While the term is sometimes used to refer to Buddhist stupas in general as a form of reverence, in Japanese architectural terminology, it refers specifically to a single-story stupa with a cylindrical central body (tōshin) and a square roof. <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten" (Dictionary of Japanese Stone Sculpture), Tokyo-do, 1978, p.347; Hisao Mae, "Kokenchiku no Kiso Chishiki" (Basic Knowledge of Ancient Architecture), Kōmura Shikōsha, 1986, p.61</ref>. The upper part of the cylindrical body is rounded in a so-called kamebara (tortoise belly) shape. A finial (sorin), similar to that on pagodas, is placed on top of the roof. Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).

{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ----
== Form and Significance of the Treasure Tower == A '''Treasure Tower''' (Hōtō) is a type of Buddhist stupa architecture. While the term is sometimes used to refer to Buddhist stupas in general as a form of reverence, in Japanese architectural terminology, it refers specifically to a single-story stupa with a cylindrical central body (tōshin) and a square roof. <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten" (Dictionary of Japanese Stone Sculpture), Tokyo-do, 1978, p.347; Hisao Mae, "Kokenchiku no Kiso Chishiki" (Basic Knowledge of Ancient Architecture), Kōmura Shikōsha, 1986, p.61</ref>. The upper part of the cylindrical body is rounded in a so-called kamebara (tortoise belly) shape. A finial (sorin), similar to that on pagodas, is placed on top of the roof. Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).

The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.

{{otheruses|Architecture|a district in Yan'an, China|Baota District}} [[File:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[Ikegami Honmonji]] Treasure Tower (National Important Cultural Property)]] The term '''Treasure Tower''' (宝塔, Hōtō) can have the following two meanings: * A type of Buddhist stupa architecture with a cylindrical main body and single-story structure, which is the focus of this article. * A term of reverence for Buddhist stupas in general. ----
== Form and Significance of the Treasure Tower == A '''Treasure Tower''' (Hōtō) is a type of Buddhist stupa architecture. While the term is sometimes used to refer to Buddhist stupas in general as a form of reverence, in Japanese architectural terminology, it refers specifically to a single-story stupa with a cylindrical central body (tōshin) and a square roof. <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten" (Dictionary of Japanese Stone Sculpture), Tokyo-do, 1978, p.347; Hisao Mae, "Kokenchiku no Kiso Chishiki" (Basic Knowledge of Ancient Architecture), Kōmura Shikōsha, 1986, p.61</ref>. The upper part of the cylindrical body is rounded in a so-called kamebara (tortoise belly) shape. A finial (sorin), similar to that on pagodas, is placed on top of the roof. Treasure Towers may be made of wood, metal, or stone, but wooden examples are exceedingly rare. The oldest surviving example in Japan is the bronze Treasure Tower found among the artifacts of the Sutra Mound at [[Kurama-dera]] Temple, dated to the Hōan era (late Heian period, around 1120). <ref>Masataro Kawakatsu, "Nihon Sekizo Bijutsu Jiten," p.347</ref>. Note that a two-story stupa with a square lower level and a circular upper level is termed a [[Multi-Treasure Tower]], though this distinction is largely a matter of convenience.
== Examples of Treasure Towers == === Wooden Treasure Towers === [[File:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|thumbnail|Stone Treasure Tower ([[Hōkaku-ji]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Yoshino District]], [[Kurotaki Village]])]] * [[Ikegami Honmonji]] (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1828 (Bunsei 11).
=== Indoor Miniature Towers === * [[Jikō-ji (Tokigawa, Saitama)|Jikō-ji]] (Saitama Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1556 (Tenbun 25). * [[Shōkaiji (Inazawa)|Shōkaiji]] (Aichi Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * [[Chōzōji (Mino, Gifu)|Chōzōji]] (Gifu Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) * Honmonji (Tokyo, Important Cultural Property) – Built in 1830 (Bunsei 13), housing a Treasure Tower within.
=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Prabhūtaratna]] * [[Seven Treasures]] * [[Kunisaki Tower]] * [[Akasaki Tower]] * [[Multi-Treasure Tower]]

=== Stone Treasure Towers === Stone sculpture forms a major category of these towers. The cylindrical main body (tōshin) is often carved with door motifs or depictions of twin Buddhas, such as [[Prabhūtaratna]] (Many-Treasures Buddha) and [[Shakyamuni]]. Notable regional examples include the [[Kunisaki Tower]] in Ōita Prefecture and the [[Akasaki Tower]] in Tottori Prefecture. * [[Kurama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Kyoto City, National Treasure) – Excavations revealed a sutra tube dated to Hōan 1 (1120). Considered the oldest extant stone Treasure Tower. * [[Sekidera]] Site "Ox Tower" (Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Late Heian period; height 3.3m. The finial was later added. * Mangan-ji Site Treasure Tower (Takashima, Shiga Prefecture) – Early Kamakura period; height 4m. The finial was later added. * [[Jōdōji (Onomichi)|Jōdōji]] Treasure Tower (Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 弘安元年 (Kōan 1, 1278); height 2.8m. * [[Saimeiji (Kōra, Shiga)|Saimeiji]] Treasure Tower (Kōra, Shiga Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Inscribed 嘉元2年 (Kagen 2, 1304). * [[Ishiyama-dera]] Stone Treasure Tower (Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture) – Mid-Kamakura period; originally 3m, now about 2m in height. * [[Anyoji (Higashiyama, Kyoto)|Anyoji]] Jien Sōjō Treasure Tower (Kyoto, Important Cultural Property) – Mid-Kamakura period; about 3m tall, with carvings of twin Buddhas. * [[Rokuharamitsuji]] Akoya Mound (Kyoto Prefecture) – Kamakura period. * [[Chōrakuji (Ōta, Gunma)|Chōrakuji]] Treasure Tower (Ōta, Gunma Prefecture, Important Cultural Property) – Dated to [[Kenji]] 2 (1276).
== References == * [[Shosaku Ishida]], "Tower: Stupa and Stūpa," Nihon no Bijutsu 77, Shibundō, 1972. * [[Toru Nakanishi]], "Nihon no Tō Sōkan" (Comprehensive Overview of Japanese Pagodas), Bunka-dō, 1970. * [[Masataro Kawakatsu]], "Sekizo Bijutsu Nyūmon" (Introduction to Stone Sculpture), Shakai Shisōsha, 1967.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Prabhūtaratna]] * [[Seven Treasures]] * [[Kunisaki Tower]] * [[Akasaki Tower]] * [[Multi-Treasure Tower]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|建築|中国延安市の区|宝塔区}} [[ファイル:池上本門寺宝塔001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|[[池上本門寺]]宝塔（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）には以下の2つの語義がある。 *仏塔の建築形式の1つで、円筒形の塔身を有する一重塔を指す。本項で説明する。 *仏塔全般の美称。 ---- == 宝塔の形態と意義 == '''宝塔'''（ほうとう）は、仏塔の建築形式の1つである。形態・形式にかかわらず、仏塔全般を指す美称として宝塔の語を用いる場合もあるが、日本建築史の用語としては、円筒形の軸部（塔身）に平面方形の屋根をもつ一重塔を指す<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347; 前久夫『古建築の基礎知識』（光村推古書院、1986）、p.61</ref>。円筒の上部を丸く面取りしいわゆる亀腹状にする。屋根の上には通常の層塔と同じく相輪を載せる。木造、金属製、石造のものがあるが、木造建築としての例は極めて少ない。現存作例としては、慈光寺開山塔、高野山瑜祇塔などがあり、金属塔では[[鞍馬寺]]経塚遺物中の銅製宝塔（保安元年頃・平安時代後期）が最古、石造では同じく鞍馬寺の平安末期の宝塔がある<ref>川勝政太郎『日本石造美術辞典』（東京堂、1978）、p.347</ref>。なお、平面方形の初重の上に平面円形の上重を載せた二重塔婆を「[[多宝塔]]」と称し、形式上「宝塔」と区別しているが、初重方形屋根は円筒部の「[[裳階]]」と考えられ、この区別は便宜的なものである。これらの形式の塔は中国にも朝鮮にも見当たらず、日本発祥の塔形と考えられている。[[空海]]が弘仁10年（819）に[[高野山]]壇上に着工、約70年をかけて完成した「根本大塔」（現況は多宝塔形式）がこの種の木造塔の最初の例とされる<ref>石田茂作編「塔 塔婆・スツーパ」には「藤原時代末ごろから発生した塔形とすべきであろう」とある。</ref>。
== 宝塔の例 == ===木造宝塔=== [[ファイル:鳳閣寺廟塔02.JPG|サムネイル|石造宝塔（[[鳳閣寺]]〈[[奈良県]][[吉野郡]][[黒滝村]]〉）]] *[[池上本門寺]]（東京都、重要文化財）1828年（文政11年）建立。
===屋内小塔=== *[[慈光寺 (埼玉県ときがわ町)|慈光寺]]（埼玉県、重要文化財）1556年(天文25年)建立。 *[[性海寺 (稲沢市)|性海寺]]（愛知県、重要文化財） *[[長蔵寺 (美濃市)|長蔵寺]]（岐阜県、重要文化財） *本門寺（東京都、重要文化財）1830年（文政13年）建立、宝塔内宝塔。
===石造宝塔=== [[石造美術]]の主要な分野を占める。塔身（円筒部）に扉型や二尊（多宝如来と釈迦如来）を刻むものがある。地方色を示す例として大分県国東の[[国東塔]]や鳥取県の[[赤碕塔]]がある。 *[[鞍馬寺]]石造宝塔（京都市・国宝） 塔の下の土中から[[保安 (元号)|保安]]元年（[[1120年]]）銘の経筒が発見された。現存最古の石造宝塔の例と考えられる。 *[[関寺]]跡「牛塔」（滋賀県・重要文化財） [[平安時代]]末期 高さ3.3m、屋根は八角形。屋根の上に載る宝殊は後補。前記鞍馬寺宝塔とともに最古に位置する。 *満願寺跡宝塔（滋賀県高島市）[[鎌倉時代]]前期 高さ4m。相輪は後補。 *[[浄土寺 (尾道市)|浄土寺]]宝塔（広島県尾道市・重要文化財） [[弘安]]元年（[[1278年]]）銘 高さ2.8m。檀上積みに立つ。 *[[西明寺 (滋賀県甲良町)|西明寺]]宝塔（滋賀県甲良町・重要文化財） [[嘉元]]2年（[[1304年]]）銘 *[[石山寺]]石造宝塔（滋賀県大津市） 鎌倉時代中期 現状高さは2mほどだが元は3mほどであったと考えられる。 *[[石山寺]]目かくし岩（滋賀県）平安時代 *[[安養寺 (京都市東山区)|安養寺]][[慈円]]僧正宝塔（京都市・重要文化財） 鎌倉時代中期 高さ約3m、塔身に多宝・釈迦二仏を刻む。 *[[六波羅蜜寺]]阿古屋塚（京都府）鎌倉時代 *[[長楽寺 (太田市)|長楽寺]]宝塔（群馬県太田市・重要文化財） [[建治]]2年（[[1276年]]）
== 参考文献 == * [[石田茂作]]「塔-塔婆・スツーバ」日本の美術77 1972 至文堂 * [[中西亨]]「日本の塔総観」1970 文華堂 * [[川勝政太郎]]「石造美術入門」1967 社会思想社
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[多宝如来]] *[[七宝]] *[[国東塔]] *[[赤城塔]] *[[多宝塔]]
